title: indulgent smoky stuffed veracruzan poblano chillies
description: a mexican veracruzan dish featuring crunchy, smoky poblano chillies stuffed with a savory satvik filling.
instructions: roast poblano chillies on a baking sheet at 400°f (200°c) for 10 minutes, turning frequently, until blistered and charred.
remove chillies from oven and let cool in a bowl covered with plastic wrap for 10 minutes.
once cool enough to handle, peel off skin, remove seeds, and cut a slit down one side of each chillie.
saute onion and garlic in oil until softened, about 5 minutes.
stir in cooked quinoa, smoked paprika, cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2 minutes.
stuff each chillie with the quinoa mixture and place seam-side down in a baking dish.
top each chillie with shredded cheese (if using) and bake at 375°f (190°c) for 10-15 minutes, until cheese is melted and bubbly.
serve immediately, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro (if desired).
